ncs_standard_definition.description_short: Designs and implements restoration plans for historical buildings and monuments, ensuring their preservation and conservation.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: A Restoration Architect is responsible for assessing the condition of historical buildings, developing restoration plans, and overseeing the execution of these plans to preserve the building's original character and integrity. They must balance the need for preservation with the need for modernization and functionality.

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in architecture and history can pursue a degree in architecture or a related field.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree in a field like engineering or fine arts can pursue a postgraduate degree in architecture or a related field.

future_builder.description: Experienced architects or conservationists can advance their careers by specializing in restoration architecture, taking on leadership roles, or starting their own practices.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 5+ years of experience as an architect or conservationist

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into restoration architecture by acquiring relevant skills and certifications, and building a portfolio of restoration projects.

roadmap.title: Restoration Techniques and Materials
resources_keywords: Indian National Trust for Art and Cultural Heritage
